xgAzOe" aria-level="2" data-attrid="title" role="heading"><b>2003 --&nbsp;</b>"Hierarchical Modeling and Analysis for Spatial Data" by&nbsp;S. Banerjee, B.P. Carlin &amp; A.E. Gelfand</div></div> </div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="110" width="120" x="1590" y="550" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-73" parent="1" style="swimlane;fontStyle=0;childLayout=stackLayout;horizontal=1;startSize=30;horizontalStack=0;resizeParent=1;resizeParentMax=0;resizeLast=0;collapsible=1;marginBottom=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="1940-1959" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="280" width="480" x="120" y="750" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-74" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-73" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>Krige works on problems in mine evaluation. His work is passed around<br/> French conferences in geostatistics, and discovered by Matheron.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="480" y="30" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-75" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-73" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>Around the same time, early problems in regression problems serve as proto-machine learning regression techniques--which will eventually be connected with stochastic processes.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="50" width="480" y="70" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-76" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-73" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>The foundations of stochastic processes having been established n the decades prior by Kolmogoroc and Weiner, early Gaussian Markov Random Field work is devloped by Whittle.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="160" width="480" y="120" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-79" parent="1" style="swimlane;fontStyle=0;childLayout=stackLayout;horizontal=1;startSize=30;horizontalStack=0;resizeParent=1;resizeParentMax=0;resizeLast=0;collapsible=1;marginBottom=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="1960-1979" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="280" width="470" x="610" y="750" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-80" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-79" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>Matheron strongly formalizes Kriging, expanding the method to universal kriging and block kriging. By the end of the 1970's, this work is made cannon through the textbook, "Mining Geostatistics" by...</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="70" width="470" y="30" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-81" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-79" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div><b>Meanwhile, Splines are connected with a Gaussian Process Prior. By the end of the 1970's, a more general regression evolves using Gaussian Processes as Bayesian Priors.</b></div><div><b><br></b></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="470" y="100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-82" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-79" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>During these decades, autoregressive models are developed in spatial contexts by Cliffa nd Ord. Coming from a separate probabilistic / computational point of view, Besag links lattices to Gaussian Markov Random Fields, developing similar models to SAR from a continuous perspective</b>." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="120" width="470" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-88" parent="1" style="swimlane;fontStyle=0;childLayout=stackLayout;horizontal=1;startSize=30;horizontalStack=0;resizeParent=1;resizeParentMax=0;resizeLast=0;collapsible=1;marginBottom=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div>2000-2020</div><div><br></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="280" width="470" x="1560" y="750" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-89" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-88" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>The O(n^3) cost of Kriging makes computation challenging, leading to several methods of cheapening the method. One approach is to use *low-rank* approximations such as in fixed-rank-kriging, while another is to use *sparse* methods, such as in the SPDE approach by Lindregen et al. Both are basis-function approaches.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="90" width="470" y="30" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-90" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-88" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>In 200something, Rasmussen and Williams publish the textbook, Gaussian Processes for Machine Learning, and "Gaussian Process Regression" further entrenches as common language for describing spatial prediction.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="30" width="470" y="120" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-91" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-88" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>Spatial modeling textbooks such as Hierarchical Modeling and Analysis for Spatial Data continue to adopt bayesian perspectives.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="130" width="470" y="150" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-96" parent="1" style="swimlane;fontStyle=0;childLayout=stackLayout;horizontal=1;startSize=30;horizontalStack=0;resizeParent=1;resizeParentMax=0;resizeLast=0;collapsible=1;marginBottom=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="1980-1999" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="280" width="470" x="1090" y="750" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-97" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-96" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>Kriging continues to appear in highly regarded spatial analysis textbooks.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="30" width="470" y="30" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-98" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-96" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>Meanwhile, Rasmussen and Williams write of Gaussian Process regression in published machine learning papers.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="30" width="470" y="60" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-99" parent="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-96" style="text;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;overflow=hidden;points=[[0,0.5],[1,0.5]];portConstraint=eastwest;rotatable=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<b>Bayesian methods see a huge boom as MCMC methods and computing resources make analysis computationally feasible. The BYM model is developed, and models built out of Krigging, Gaussian Processes, SAR and CAR models see an explosion in use.</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="190" width="470" y="90" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-77" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;dashed=1;dashPattern=12 12;" value="<div><b>Some foundational mathematics for stochastic processes, e.g.&nbsp;</b></div><div><b>"Interpolation and Extrapolation" by N. Kolmororov in 1941 and "Extrapolation, Interpolation, and Smoothing of Stationary Time Series" in 1949 by N. Weiner</b></div><div><b><br></b></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="120" width="230" x="100" y="550" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="_CWQq-FcRHTgxDXuN05n-106" edge="1" parent="1" div.b: Matheron strongly formalizes Kriging, expanding the method to universal kriging and block kriging. By the end of the 1970's, this work is made cannon through the textbook, "Mining Geostatistics" by...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 460px; height: 1px; padding-top: 866px; margin-left: 566px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 56px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.b: Meanwhile, Splines are connected with a Gaussian Process Prior. By the end of the 1970's, a more general regression evolves using Gaussian Processes as Bayesian Priors.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 460px; height: 1px; padding-top: 956px; margin-left: 566px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 116px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: During these decades, autoregressive models are developed in spatial contexts by Cliffa nd Ord. Coming from a separate probabilistic / computational point of view, Besag links lattices to Gaussian Markov Random Fields, developing similar models to SAR from a continuous perspective.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 468px; height: 1px; padding-top: 751px; margin-left: 1511px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;

display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 460px; height: 1px; padding-top: 811px; margin-left: 1516px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 86px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.b: The O(n^3) cost of Kriging makes computation challenging, leading to several methods of cheapening the method. One approach is to use *low-rank* approximations such as in fixed-rank-kriging, while another is to use *sparse* methods, such as in the SPDE approach by Lindregen et al. Both are basis-function approaches.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 460px; height: 1px; padding-top: 871px; margin-left: 1516px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 26px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.b: In 200something, Rasmussen and Williams publish the textbook, Gaussian Processes for Machine Learning, and "Gaussian Process Regression" further entrenches as common language for describing spatial prediction.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 460px; height: 1px; padding-top: 951px; margin-left: 1516px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 126px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.b: Spatial modeling textbooks such as Hierarchical Modeling and Analysis for Spatial Data continue to adopt bayesian perspectives.

div.b: Kriging continues to appear in highly regarded spatial analysis textbooks.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 460px; height: 1px; padding-top: 811px; margin-left: 1046px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 26px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;

div.b: Meanwhile, Rasmussen and Williams write of Gaussian Process regression in published machine learning papers.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 460px; height: 1px; padding-top: 921px; margin-left: 1046px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 186px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.b: Bayesian methods see a huge boom as MCMC methods and computing resources make analysis computationally feasible. The BYM model is developed, and models built out of Krigging, Gaussian Processes, SAR and CAR models see an explosion in use.

div.b: Some foundational mathematics for stochastic processes, e.g.
"Interpolation and Extrapolation" by N. Kolmororov in 1941 and "Extrapolation, Interpolation, and Smoothing of Stationary Time Series" in 1949 by N. Weiner
